raw_consumer_statement:
We attended a XXXX sales presentation in XXXX, FL on XX/XX/year>. We were shown only a XXXX-page Statement of Understanding on an iPad about a XXXX XXXX program at $ XXXX. We initialed that XXXX page. No total price, contract terms, or {$10000.00} charge were shown or disclosed. 

After we initialed that page, the XXXX representative took back the device and completed the rest of the documents himself using XXXX host controls. We did not receive a XXXX email, did not have access to the XXXX-page contract at signing, and did not approve the {$10000.00} charge on the XXXX Barclays card . 

We later discovered the charge and immediately disputed it in writing. Barclays later sent a letter saying they attempted to reach us for more information and closed the dispute. We checked our phones and email and did not receive any contact. We believe the dispute was closed before our notarized documents were added to the file. 

We called Barclays immediately, and the representative confirmed our documents are now in the case file and instructed us to submit a written request to reopen. We faxed that request. XXXX has only told us verbally that we are liable and said they would follow up with this decision by email ; we have not received any written decision from them. We are also filing a police report for misuse of our electronic signature and will provide it when available. We are asking Barclays to reopen the dispute, pause charges and credit reporting, and conduct a full review of the evidence.
